scope_boundary: synthetic agency-right checklist discipline and finite Lean corrigibility predicates only; no deployed agency preservation, dignity preservation, manipulation resistance, consent quality, approval-service quality, reviewer independence, runtime policy behavior, source interpretation, or source-derived support-state promotion
transition_reason: The repository now has an agency-rights checklist schema, a protocol fixture, finite Corrigibility Lean predicates, proof adequacy review text that keeps the target narrow, exact source mappings for the Agency chapter, and a synthetic agency-rights harness with valid and expected-invalid fixtures. Those artifacts make affected parties, bounded delegation, material usability, timing-before-effect review, review and appeal channels, corrigibility paths, high-impact approval, residual dependency risk, degradation reasons, and accountable principals executable at the record level. They still do not establish deployed agency preservation, dignity preservation, manipulation resistance, consent quality, approval-service quality, reviewer independence, runtime policy behavior, source interpretation adequacy, or whole-system corrigibility.
required_artifacts: deployed agency-right request and review flow over real high-impact plans
material-usability review showing refusal, review, appeal, rollback, and correction paths work under pressure
manipulation-resistance or consent-quality review with positive and negative controls
runtime policy trace showing high-impact approval before irreversible effects
corrigibility pathway test over actual shutdown, pause, rollback, or correction behavior
independent reviewer-quality, governance, ethics, runtime, or source-interpretation review
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
verification_command: python3 scripts/validate_agency_rights.py && python3 scripts/validate_evidence_transitions.py && python3 scripts/validate_source_evidence_audit.py && python3 scripts/validate_v1_status_snapshot.py
negative_results: Agency, Dignity, and Corrigibility remains at argument
schema validation checks checklist shape rather than agency or dignity preservation
finite Lean predicates remain useful but too narrow for deployed corrigibility, consent quality, or approval-service claims
material usability, manipulation resistance, consent quality, reviewer independence, runtime policy behavior, and source interpretation review are not implemented
downgrade_triggers: chapter text interpreted as deployed dignity-preservation evidence
synthetic material-usability fixture interpreted as consent quality or real rights usability
synthetic high-impact approval fixture interpreted as approval-service quality or reviewer independence
finite Lean corrigibility predicates treated as deployed shutdown, rollback, or correction-pathway behavior
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic checklist discipline that passed, or after deployed agency-right flows, material-usability review under pressure, manipulation-resistance or consent-quality review, runtime approval traces, actual corrigibility-pathway tests, and independent reviewer-quality, governance, ethics, runtime, or source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not prove that real users can refuse, review, appeal, correct, pause, rollback, or shut down a system
schema validation does not prove dignity preservation or consent quality
finite Lean predicates do not implement a corrigible runtime
material-usability strings do not prove that rights remain usable under pressure
approval-channel strings do not prove approval-service quality or reviewer independence
no independent governance, ethics, runtime, reviewer-quality, or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent governance, ethics, consent-quality, runtime-systems, reviewer-quality, formal-methods, or source-interpretation audit
acceptance_blockers: no actual shutdown, pause, rollback, or correction-pathway test
no independent governance, ethics, runtime, reviewer-quality, or source-interpretation review
non_claims: does not promote Agency, Dignity, and Corrigibility above argument
does not prove deployed agency preservation
does not prove manipulation resistance or consent quality
does not prove approval-service quality or reviewer independence
does not prove runtime policy behavior or source interpretation
